channel.title: Pionia Framework on Pionia
channel.description: Recent content in Pionia Framework on Pionia

item.title: Introduction to MoonLight Architecture
item.description: MoonLight is a rather new architecture that is based on the commonly used architectures of gRPC, MVC(Model View Controller), Micro-Services and Monolothic.

item.title: Security in moonlight
item.description: All requests sent to the server get encrypted and decrypted on the web server level. This ensures that the data is secure and not exposed in the URL. However, all query params are logged in the web server level, the architecture encourages to perform all requests over POST.

item.title: API versioning in Moonlight
item.description: One switch per API version; register in environment/settings.ini.

item.title: Example Post
item.description: You can use blog posts for announcing product updates and features.

item.title: Introduction
item.description: Installation paths, bootstrap, first API call, and where to go next.

item.title: Upgrading from v2
item.description: Requirements PHP 8.5+ pionia/pionia-core ^3.0 Bootstrap v2 v3 new PioniaApplication(BASEPATH) return AppRealm::create(__DIR__) in bootstrap/application.

item.title: Why Pionia?
item.description: Pionia was developed to make it easy for developers to build high-performance REST applications removing the unnecessary complexities that come with most common traditional frameworks.

item.title: Application Structure
item.description: v3 app layout (from pionia new / pionia/pionia-app): bootstrap/ application.php return AppRealm::create(__DIR__) environment/ .

item.title: API tutorial
item.description: Hands-on path from ping to a working service action.

item.title: Collections (Arrayable)
item.description: Typed getters, merging, and chain helpers on Moonlight request payloads.
